actual_result: Commit 'PROP-007: Add build.js and build-scripts utilities to OWNERSHIP table' on 2026-04-16T17:02:03Z added the following to build.js OWNERSHIP: 'build-scripts/digest-reviews.js', 'build-scripts/fix-json-quotes.js', 'build-scripts/archive-old-reports.js', and 'build.js' itself. The implementation used a narrower subset than the proposal suggested (the proposal listed 6 build-scripts; the implementation added only the 3 actively-used ones plus build.js self-sync). Verification pattern returns FIXED. FUSE staleness cascade from yesterday's report has collapsed — all 5 files in the quick-check md5 loop now match main.
target: build.js OWNERSHIP table + CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules
title: Classify build.js and all build-scripts/*.js as git-owned so publish keeps workspace in sync
problem:
The workspace→git publish sync loop iterates `OWNERSHIP` keys only. `build.js` itself and every `build-scripts/*.js` file except `generate-html.js`/`generate-pdf.js` are unclassified, so no publish ever copies them into the FUSE workspace. Consequences observed this run (2026-04-15T09:39Z):

1. `build.js` in workspace has md5 c3ad8c95, GitHub main has dbd0e96c. Last GitHub commit to build.js was 2026-04-10T17:50:45Z — five days of stale workspace copy, right through the Phase-1 OWNERSHIP-coverage fix that added `data/predictions.json`, `docs/llms.txt`, `docs/sitemap.xml`, `docs/robots.txt`.
2. `build-scripts/digest-reviews.js` in workspace has md5 0a11b762, GitHub main has 6a742c5e. Last commit was 2026-04-15T05:23:37Z — 4 hours ago — and that commit is exactly the silent-parse-error fix (exit code 2, verbose errors). Any integrity-agent run that actually invokes the workspace-rooted `node build-scripts/digest-reviews.js --workspace .` will execute the old version without the exit-code signal.
3. Previous tinker runs (2026-04-13, 2026-04-14) grep'd the workspace build.js and reported `OWNERSHIP misalignment: data/predictions.json, docs/llms.txt, ... miss` as STILL_BROKEN. The bug was fixed on main on 2026-04-10 but the workspace copy never received the update — so the tinker report has been chasing a ghost for two consecutive runs.

Six more build-scripts are in the same boat and are one edit away from a similar silent staleness bug: `add-references.js`, `apply-patches.js`, `backfill-issues.js`, `extract-sections.js`, `sync-code-analysis.js`, plus `build.js` itself. They currently happen to be in sync only because nothing has edited them since their last manual workspace copy.

The CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules list is also incomplete on this point — it mentions only `generate-html.js` and `generate-pdf.js` as 'infrastructure', implying the rest live outside the classification system.

proposed_fix.summary: Add every build-scripts/*.js file and `build.js` itself to the OWNERSHIP table as git-owned. build.js self-sync is safe because fs.copyFileSync(src,dst) with src==dst where FUSE can't unlink is still a legal overwrite; the existing `copyIfExists` helper already handles mkdirSync for parents so it is idempotent. Then mirror the addition in CLAUDE.md's File Ownership Rules list so the three authoritative locations (build.js OWNERSHIP, workspace-sync.md OWNED_BY_GIT, CLAUDE.md) stay consistent — per CLAUDE.md's own rule: 'If you edit one, edit all three.'

files_to_modify.replace:
'build-scripts/generate-html.js': 'git',
  'build-scripts/generate-pdf.js': 'git',
  'build-scripts/digest-reviews.js': 'git',
  'build-scripts/sync-code-analysis.js': 'git',
  'build-scripts/add-references.js': 'git',
  'build-scripts/apply-patches.js': 'git',
  'build-scripts/backfill-issues.js': 'git',
  'build-scripts/extract-sections.js': 'git',
  'build.js': 'git',                       // self-sync: publish copies its own source to workspace
files_to_modify.explanation: Classifies every active build script (and build.js itself) as git-owned. The publish-loop's copyIfExists(relPath) is idempotent when src and dst are distinct paths — the workspace mount is a different filesystem than the clean clone, so self-sync of build.js is a cross-filesystem overwrite and FUSE's no-unlink restriction does not apply (copyFileSync uses truncate+write, not unlink+rename).

Mirrors the OWNERSHIP extension. workspace-sync uses OWNED_BY_GIT to short-circuit any workspace→git push of these files (they are authoritative in git, so if the workspace copy ever diverges it's always because the workspace is stale, never because it is newer).

files_to_modify.replace: - `build-scripts/generate-html.js`, `build-scripts/generate-pdf.js`, `build-scripts/digest-reviews.js`, `build-scripts/sync-code-analysis.js`, `build-scripts/add-references.js`, `build-scripts/apply-patches.js`, `build-scripts/backfill-issues.js`, `build-scripts/extract-sections.js`, `build.js` — infrastructure (all git-owned; publish copies into workspace so agents running from FUSE see the same version that's on main).
files_to_modify.explanation: Keeps the CLAUDE.md file-ownership reference honest. Without this, a future editor will read CLAUDE.md, assume only the two generator scripts are classified, and the drift will recur.

tradeoffs:
GAIN: The workspace stops drifting on every commit that touches build.js or any other build-script. That kills the class of false-positive tinker reports that have been escalating the OWNERSHIP-misalignment finding (fixed on main since 2026-04-10, still flagged in workspace-backed checks). It also hardens the integrity agent's check 7f — if integrity ever actually invokes `node build-scripts/digest-reviews.js --workspace .` from the FUSE mount, it will now run the version with the exit-code-2 signal that check 7f relies on.

LOSE: One more file-ownership claim to maintain. If a new build script is added and not registered, it will silently fall out of sync again — but that's the current failure mode anyway. Self-syncing build.js is a mild foot-gun: if someone ever introduces a build.js bug that crashes mid-publish after `git push` but before the sync block, the workspace will run an old build.js on the next attempt and succeed where a fresh build.js would have failed. That is probably a feature, not a bug, because it makes publish self-healing, but it does mean the workspace build.js is never a strict witness for 'what's on main'.

The counter-case — leave this alone — is weaker than it looks. The argument would be: 'no agent actually runs build.js or digest-reviews.js from the workspace, so staleness is cosmetic.' That's almost true today, but it gambles on a fragile invariant. CLAUDE.md explicitly says 'scheduled agents' read prompt files from FUSE; extending the same expectation to build-scripts is a small leap that a future prompt edit could make silently. Better to close the gap now.
estimated_impact: Removes a recurring false-positive class from tinker reports (2 runs already wasted on it). Prevents a latent bug where integrity runs the stale digest-reviews.js and misses the exit-code signal. Token cost: ~300 chars of OWNERSHIP text, one-time.
verification_pattern: grep -q "'build-scripts/digest-reviews.js': 'git'" build.js && grep -q "'build.js': 'git'" build.js && echo FIXED || echo STILL_BROKEN

escalation_history.note: First escalation — root cause of OWNERSHIP-misalignment false positive in 04-13 and 04-14 tinker reports.
